steps.caption: Welcome to the DISH POS dashboard. In this tutorial, we will show how to adjust product details.
First, go to Articles.
Then click on Products.
To edit or add product information, click on the editing icon. The following steps also apply to a new product, by first clicking + Add product.
Using the corresponding text field, fill in the name of the product.
Through the drop-down menu, you can assign the product to a product group.
At Turnover categories, select the corresponding turnover group using the drop-down menu. If it differs from the default, you can change it.
At Prices, you can set your price information.
Enter the price of the product.
Click into the respective text field to change the option price of the product.
Click the drop-down menu to select your desired tax rate. Note: VAT must be different to the default.
If a product has a price per unit, set the quantity and unit with the corresponding text field and drop-down menu.
Products are displayed with short names in the app. To give your product a short name, use the respective text field.
To add a barcode or another reference, use the text fields SearchField1 and SearchField2.
At Production you can adjust the settings of the production flow for this product.
At Production Order, set the importance by entering a number. Production Order 1 is printed before Production Order 2.
If the default production facility (printer/screen) differs, select another by clicking + Add production attributes.
To set up a pop-up for the option menu, click + Add option menu.
If the product contains composites, set those components by clicking + Add component.
To give the product a description, go to Product description.
Then use the respective text field to enter a description of the product.
To add images, click on Images.
Use the + Upload image field to attach a picture to your product.
Upload your image by clicking Select file, or by dragging it into the zone. Only .jpg, .png, .jpeg up to 4 MB are supported.
Once you uploaded your image, click on Save.
Back in the images overview, under the Apply column use the drop-down menu to set where the image is applied.
To assign allergens and additives to the product, click on Product Information.
Then check the boxes for allergens and additives that are relevant to your product.
Once you are done, click on Save to apply your changes. That's it - you now know how to adjust product details.
